headline.text: L1 Sollich 2 Cavanna 3 was down *4.5 hours* on sensor faults — cleared at 16:11, then MM6 issues stopped the line early overnight.

per_plant_summary.L1: Sollich 2 cleared a 4.5-hour Cavanna 3 outage at 16:11 after turning off the reject sensor and adjusting film/jaw position. Overnight, MM6 sensor failures and matrix issues across all three units forced the line to stop early and send caramel to rework. Sollich 3 ran with frequent pauses for caramel — cook times spiked to 94 minutes, requiring multiple line stops to replenish reserve.
per_plant_summary.L2: FFS cleared a brief high-weight reject spike by realigning the incline belt. QA flagged a supersack with mixed lots (61AUC label, 61BUC product inside) and placed it on hold for rework. Grinding reported high waste on Brookie pieces under 1 inch; RCA meeting scheduled for Tuesday.
per_plant_summary.L3: Truffle Line failed Level 2 clean on Friday (cooling tunnels, belts) — team re-cleaned overnight and passed ATP by 01:04. ML1 chain ran well all shift. ML4 WDS cleared errors and ran at 20 mpm with no issues by midday.

resolved.title: Cavanna 3 down on sensor faults and film/jaw errors
resolved.summary: Cavanna 3 threw OMAC alarms and belt faults at 11:38, tried multiple resets and reject air adjustments with vendor support, finally cleared by turning off reject sensor, adjusting film/jaw position, and reducing speed from 700 to 600.
resolved.resolution: Cleared at 16:11 — "seems to be running well, the OMAC alarm hasn't returned" after turning off reject sensor, adjusting film/jaw, and lowering speed to 600 (Caleb York). Both Cavannas confirmed running well by 21:27.

resolved.title: Chocolate feet on caramels from tunnel/enrober settings
resolved.summary: Water table and fans for tunnel 2 were turned off, and enrober blower was on when it should be off, preventing chocolate from solidifying before enrobing.
resolved.resolution: Settings corrected (water table/fans on, blower off) — "product looks good" by 08:46 (Katya Farnsworth).

resolved.title: Guide rail falling before Cavanna 4, loose bolt causing jams
resolved.summary: Operator saw guide rail repeatedly falling and pieces on the floor; inspection found a loose bolt.
resolved.resolution: Bolt tightened at 08:27 — "everything is running well right now" (Katya Farnsworth).

resolved.title: S3 ran out of caramel at 16:15, reduced line speed
resolved.summary: Caramel reserve depleted due to rising cook times (C2 at 76 minutes); line paused and speed reduced from 58 to 56.
resolved.resolution: Caramel sent to line by 16:51 — "Line 3 is running with caramel" (Conner Boyd).

resolved.title: 6 pallets of MM678 with incorrect box labels (item 30892)
resolved.summary: IMS and QA confusion led to wrong item number printed on 6 pallets of MM678 pouches.
resolved.resolution: All 6 pallets re-labeled by 19:07 and tagged to prevent warehouse movement — "I identified them with a Tag so that Warehouse wouldn't move them" (Angelica Perez). Rework confirmed complete at 23:23.

resolved.title: Double-sided tape shortage delayed Cavanna roll change
resolved.summary: Ran out of double-sided tape for a quick Cavanna roll change, triggering a 30-minute pause to pass accumulation.
resolved.resolution: Joey delivered tape by 07:27 — "Done!" (Joey Michael Dudney).

resolved.title: FFS high-weight rejects on incline belt
resolved.summary: Checkweigher rejected a burst of high-weight bags; QA verified all were normal weight.
resolved.resolution: All bags passed second check at 19:24; incline belt found slightly south, realigned — "bags seem to be centered on the incline belt for the most part" (Mathew Draper).

resolved.title: Truffle Line Level 2 clean failed ATP on tunnels and belts
resolved.summary: QA did not pass sections on Cavanna and north tunnel after Friday's Level 2 clean; team had already left for the day.
resolved.resolution: Re-clean completed overnight, passed ATP by 01:04 — "Level 2 clean completed and passed off ATP" (Raoul Lubain).

resolved.title: ML4 WDS errors during recirculation
resolved.summary: WDS threw errors during startup testing with Alex (Theegarten tech) on-site.
resolved.resolution: Errors cleared by 13:43 — "WDS is now running at 20mpm for 10min with no issues" (Itzel Chavez). Shutdown checklist completed by 14:59.

needs_attention.title: MM6 sensor failure, all three matrixes causing scrap and jams
needs_attention.summary: MM6 sensor on top not functioning correctly; all three matrixes (especially MM6) caused heavy accumulation, pouch scrap, and forced early line stop with caramel sent to rework buckets.
needs_attention.status: Maintenance worked all night but could not fix MM6. Line stopped early at 06:57 and caramel sent to rework. Needs attention before Monday production — flagged to Justin Garner and Chris Givens.

needs_attention.title: S3 caramel cook times spiked to 94 minutes, multiple pauses
needs_attention.summary: Cook times climbed from 47/66 minutes to 94/91 minutes over the shift, forcing three separate line pauses to replenish caramel reserve and progressive speed reductions from 58 to 50.
needs_attention.status: Line restarted at 40 dpm at 00:31 after final pause. Cook times remain elevated; third shift noted possible caustic wash needed for second shift Monday.

needs_attention.title: Sollich 2 valve gasket blown out, cap workaround failed
needs_attention.summary: Gasket on valve under the reserve tank blew out; capping solution caused another blowout.
needs_attention.status: Temporary fix in place; valve gasket replacement needed when able. Last mention at 16:56, still on workaround at window close.

needs_attention.title: Leak under S3 C2 kettle water valve
needs_attention.summary: Water valve directly under C2 kettle is leaking.
needs_attention.status: Maintenance informed and aware at 09:00. No resolution posted.

needs_attention.title: S3 kitchen Tempek chiller loud noise and water leak
needs_attention.summary: Tempek chiller making unfamiliar loud noise with water leaking from underneath; all kitchen temperatures and caramel temps reading normal.
needs_attention.status: Maintenance informed at 12:23, no update by window close. Temps stable, line running.

needs_attention.title: Brookie pieces under 1 inch driving high waste in Grinding
needs_attention.summary: Grinding reported elevated waste on Brookie pieces smaller than 1 inch, spec compliance concern.
needs_attention.status: Team coaching in progress on verifying correct grinder settings. RCA meeting scheduled no later than Tuesday with Brandon Lance, Gustavo Rodas, Hervin Herrera, and Alan Todd.

needs_attention.title: Supersack with two different lot labels (61AUC / 61BUC mixed)
needs_attention.summary: Supersack brought for Lot 61AUC line had two labels; inspection revealed product inside is actually Lot 61BUC, not 61AUC.
needs_attention.status: QA placed entire supersack on hold/rework at 22:07. Alan Todd escalated to Rose Smuin at 22:12 — "We can't mix lots in supersacks." Verification in progress.
